turns.user_message: My chest gets this tight, heavy feeling that just sits right behind my ribs. I used to just shrug it off and keep going with whatever I was doing, but lately it lingers longer than it should. It feels like a rubber band slowly stretching around my lungs, making every breath feel a little shallow. I try to ignore it while I sit on the couch, but the sensation just keeps pulling my attention inward. It is honestly exhausting to fight through this weird pressure when I just want to relax.

That same tightness in my chest has become a constant background noise that I cannot seem to shake. It used to be a fleeting thing, but now it shows up almost every single evening without fail. I catch myself holding my breath without even realizing it, waiting for the pressure to finally ease up. My shoulders stay locked up near my ears and my hands get clammy even when the room is cool. I keep telling myself it will pass, but the heavy weight just refuses to lift and it drains all my energy.

The heavy pressure in my chest has completely taken over my entire body and I feel completely trapped inside my own skin. What started as a minor annoyance has turned into this relentless wave that crashes over me whenever I try to settle down. My heart starts pounding so hard it echoes in my ears, and my legs feel like they are made of wet cement. I pace around the house trying to shake off the suffocating grip, but the sensation just tightens its hold. I am so tired of feeling like I am constantly bracing for impact when nothing is even happening.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This used to not bother the speaker.
